task_completion.description: Create 'PROFILE' and 'CENTERS' layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows a 'PROFILE' layer (white/default color) and a 'CENTERS' layer with a red color indicator.

task_completion.description: Draw the head and handle end circles
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main drawing canvas displays two circles: a larger one on the left (head) and a smaller one on the right (handle end), spaced horizontally.

task_completion.description: Draw tangent lines connecting the two circles
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two straight lines connect the top and bottom outer edges of the large and small circles, forming the tapered handle of the wrench.

task_completion.description: Draw the jaw opening on the head circle
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two parallel horizontal lines extend leftwards from the center area of the large circle, creating the open-ended jaw of the wrench.

task_completion.description: Draw the horizontal centerline in the 'CENTERS' layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red horizontal line spans across the middle of the entire wrench shape, passing through the centers of both circles.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing as din894_wrench.dxf in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar, a save dialog, or the command line history shows the file saved as 'din894_wrench.dxf' in '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/'.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD interface with active tool selections, command line inputs within the CAD software, or cursor interactions, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The drawing consists of actual CAD vector elements
integrity.visual_evidence: The shapes on the canvas appear as standard CAD lines and circles with selection handles or snap points, not as an imported raster image of a wrench.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to download or copy a pre-made DXF file
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show commands like 'wget', 'curl', or 'cp' being used to place a pre-existing 'din894_wrench.dxf' file into the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/din894_wrench.dxf. The drawing must contain two layers: 'PROFILE' (White) and 'CENTERS' (Red). The geometry includes a head circle (center 0,0; radius 26.0), a tail circle (center 215,0; radius 13.0), and a jaw opening with a Y offset of 12.0 and -12.0. The centerline should be horizontal along the X-axis.
